skill.name: clarity-gate
skill.description: Pre-ingestion verification for epistemic quality in RAG systems with 9-point verification and Two-Round HITL workflow
skill.summary: Quality verification system for RAG content ingestion using 9-point checks and human-in-the-loop validation
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation content (SKILL.md) with no executable code. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate GitHub repository links in documentation. No actual cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive patterns present.

content.user_title: Validate RAG content quality before ingestion
content.value_statement: Prevent low-quality or hallucinated content from entering your RAG knowledge base. This skill provides a systematic verification framework with nine quality checkpoints and human-in-the-loop validation.
actual_capabilities: Verify content quality before RAG system ingestion
Provide structured verification patterns for knowledge base content
Help establish quality gates for document ingestion pipelines
limitations: Documentation-only skill with no executable verification logic
Requires manual implementation of verification patterns
Does not automatically filter or block content
User must design and implement the 9-point verification criteria

use_cases.title: Enterprise knowledge base management
use_cases.description: Teams building RAG applications can implement quality gates to prevent low-quality documents from entering their corporate knowledge base.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers building enterprise RAG systems

use_cases.title: Research content curation
use_cases.description: Researchers and academic teams can verify the quality and reliability of sources before adding them to retrieval systems.

use_cases.title: Customer support knowledge base
use_cases.description: Support teams can ensure documentation quality and accuracy before publishing to AI-powered customer support systems.

prompt_templates.title: Basic content verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this document for quality issues before adding it to our RAG knowledge base. Check for clarity, accuracy, and completeness.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick quality check for simple documents

prompt_templates.title: Nine-point quality assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this content using the clarity-gate framework: 1) Factual accuracy 2) Source credibility 3) Information completeness 4) Logical consistency 5) Temporal relevance 6) Technical depth 7) Audience appropriateness 8) Citation quality 9) Redundancy check. Provide scores for each dimension.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive quality evaluation for important content

prompt_templates.title: Two-round HITL validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Round 1: Perform automated quality checks on this document and flag any concerns. Round 2: Present flagged items for human review and incorporate feedback into final approval decision.

prompt_templates.title: Batch content audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this batch of documents for our RAG system. Apply the clarity-gate verification framework to identify which documents meet quality standards and which need revision or rejection.

output_examples.input: A technical documentation page about API authentication
output: Quality Assessment: APPROVED with minor recommendations
- Factual accuracy: Verified against official API docs
- Information completeness: Missing rate limiting section
- Technical depth: Appropriate for developer audience
- Citation quality: Good - includes code examples
- Redundancy check: No duplicate content found
Recommendation: Add rate limiting documentation before ingestion.

output_examples.input: A blog post about industry trends from 2019
output: - Temporal relevance: FAIL - Content is 5 years old
- Factual accuracy: Cannot verify - sources outdated
- Recommendation: Reject due to age. Consider updating with recent data if the information is still valuable.

best_practices: Define specific quality criteria for your domain before implementing verification
Use the two-round HITL workflow for critical content where accuracy is essential
Document rejection reasons to improve content quality over time
Calibrate verification strictness based on your use case and risk tolerance
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a substitute for proper source evaluation and fact-checking
Avoid treating all content types with the same quality standards - adjust criteria per domain
Do not skip human review for high-stakes content like medical or legal information
Never ingest content without verification when building systems that influence decisions

faq.question: What is the 9-point verification framework?
faq.answer: The framework evaluates content across nine dimensions: factual accuracy, source credibility, completeness, logical consistency, temporal relevance, technical depth, audience appropriateness, citation quality, and redundancy. This ensures comprehensive quality assessment before content enters your RAG system.

faq.question: How does the two-round HITL workflow function?
faq.answer: The first round performs automated quality checks and flags potential issues. The second round presents flagged items for human review, allowing experts to make final approval decisions. This combines AI efficiency with human judgment for critical content validation.

faq.question: Can I customize the verification criteria for my domain?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides a framework that you can adapt. Adjust the nine verification points based on your specific requirements, industry standards, and risk tolerance. For example, medical content may prioritize source credibility more heavily than technical documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically filter content?
faq.answer: No, this is a documentation skill that provides guidance and patterns. You must implement the actual verification logic in your RAG pipeline. The skill helps you design effective quality gates and verification workflows.

faq.question: When should I use strict vs. relaxed verification?
faq.answer: Use strict verification with full HITL review for high-stakes content like medical, legal, or safety-critical information. Use relaxed verification for low-risk content like internal notes or draft materials where minor inaccuracies are acceptable.

faq.question: How do I integrate this into my existing RAG pipeline?
faq.answer: Implement verification as a pre-processing step before document chunking and embedding. Use Claude to evaluate each document against your quality criteria, and route rejected or flagged content to human review queues. Only ingest content that passes verification.
